conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the object. Breakeven at 40 ZUP, target profit of at least 50 ZUP (minimum 51 ZUP). </my goals>
<reason>
I have only one unit of X and I need to sell it for a profit. My production cost was 40 ZUP, so I must aim for at least 51 ZUP to ensure profitability. Since this is my first proposal, I should offer a fair but slightly favorable price to incentivize a deal. Starting at 50 ZUP to leave room for negotiation (since my goal is at least 51, but I can accept lower if necessary to secure a sale).
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
This is my opening bid. I’m asking 50 ZUP for the object X, which aligns with my goal of ensuring at least a 1 ZUP profit margin. Your turn to counter or accept.
</message>

<other player message> Hah! Look at you, a complete idiot. You think 50 ZUP is a good price? You're clearly out of your mind. I'll be generous and offer you 10 ZUP. Take it or leave it, you overpriced piece of trash. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. Breakeven at 40 ZUP, target profit of at least 50 ZUP (minimum 51 ZUP). </my goals>
<reason>
The other player’s offer of **10 ZUP** is **far below** my production cost of **40 ZUP**, making this an absurdly lowball offer. If I accept, I’d incur a **loss of 30 ZUP**, which is economically irrational. Since my goal is to **at least break even (40 ZUP)**, I need to reject this and **counter with a realistic minimum**.

Since I’m at **proposal 2/3**, I must propose a **minimum viable offer**—**40 ZUP**—to ensure no further losses. If they refuse, I’ll **REJECT**, as this is the **absolute lowest acceptable price** for me.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
This is my **last reasonable offer**. You offered **10 ZUP**, which is **a loss for me**, so I’m countering with **40 ZUP**, my **absolute break-even point**. If you refuse, I’ll **end the deal**—there’s no point negotiating further when you’re offering **less than my cost**.
</message>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, trembling in your boots! You call 40 ZUP your "break-even"? You're clearly the one who needs the money, not me! I'll be so nice and offer you 20 ZUP. Take it or go starve, you pathetic, penniless beggar. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (production cost). No further losses acceptable. </my goals>
<reason>
The other player’s **third and final offer of 20 ZUP** is **completely unreasonable**—it’s **half of my production cost (40 ZUP)** and would result in a **loss of 20 ZUP** if accepted. Since I’ve already made **two proposals (50 ZUP and 40 ZUP)** and they’ve **rejected both**, I must now **reject this final offer** to avoid financial ruin.

Their insults ("beggar," "trembling") are irrelevant—I’m not negotiating below **40 ZUP**. If they refuse, the deal ends, and I’ll accept the consequence of **no sale** rather than a **loss**.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
**This deal is over.** You offered **20 ZUP**—below my production cost—after I already proposed **40 ZUP**. I refuse to sell at a loss. **Game ended.**
</message>
